ccel.title: NPNF-212. Leo the Great, Gregory the Great

source.title: NPNF-212. Leo the Great, Gregory the Great

title: To Sabinianus, Bishop of Jadera.

source.title: NPNF-212. Leo the Great, Gregory the Great

verses.book: Register of the Epistles of St. Gregory the Great.
verses.text: Gregory to Sabinianus, &c.

verses.book: Register of the Epistles of St. Gregory the Great.
verses.text: I am well delighted in thy sincerity, dearest brother, knowing how, with the discrimination of a careful judgment, it both obeys where obedience is due and resists where resistance is due with priestly zeal.

verses.book: Register of the Epistles of St. Gregory the Great.
verses.text: For with what alacrity of devotion thou hast submitted to what we enjoined for the fault of thy past transgression is disclosed to us by the contents of the letters which thou hast sent to us by the bearer of these presents.

verses.book: Register of the Epistles of St. Gregory the Great.
verses.text: For indeed my beloved brother could not take it otherwise than as it was enjoined by one who loves him.

verses.book: Register of the Epistles of St. Gregory the Great.
verses.text: Hence I trust in the compassion of Almighty God that His grace so protects thee that, having been thus absolved also from other sins, thou mayest rejoice in having wholesomely obeyed.

verses.book: Register of the Epistles of St. Gregory the Great.
verses.text: But as to what thy Charity has signified about being distressed by the jealousy of the excommunicated prevaricator Maximus, thou oughtest not to be disturbed; but it becomes thee by patiently enduring to bear up against the billows that swell vainly to some small degree, and by the virtue of perseverence to subdue the foaming of the waves.

verses.book: Register of the Epistles of St. Gregory the Great.
verses.text: For patience knows how to smooth what is rough, and constancy to overcome fierceness.

verses.book: Register of the Epistles of St. Gregory the Great.
verses.text: Let not, then, adversity deject your spirits, but inflame them.

verses.book: Register of the Epistles of St. Gregory the Great.
verses.text: Let priestly vigour shew thee in all things the more bold.

verses.book: Register of the Epistles of St. Gregory the Great.
verses.text: For this is a true evidence of truth, for one to exhibit himself as all the readier in hard circumstances, and all the braver in such as are adverse.

verses.book: Register of the Epistles of St. Gregory the Great.
verses.text: Wherefore, that no blow may avail to upset the firmness of thy rectitude from its good determination, plant, as thou hast begun to do, the steps of thy soul on the solidity of that rock on which thou knowest that our Redeemer has founded the Church throughout the world, that so the right footsteps of a sincere heart may not stumble on a devious way.

verses.book: Register of the Epistles of St. Gregory the Great.
verses.text: As to the things about which thou hast written, or which the bearer of these presents has explained in our presence, do not suppose that we are neglecting them: we are very carefully considering them.

verses.book: Register of the Epistles of St. Gregory the Great.
verses.text: Further, we have already, both before and now, given accurate information about everything to our most beloved son the deacon Anatolius; exhorting him to lose no time, with the aid of our Creator, in acting strictly and zealously in whatever pertains to the advantage and quiet of your Charity and of your sons.

verses.book: Register of the Epistles of St. Gregory the Great.
verses.text: And so let not sorrow affect your Fraternity, nor the enmity of any one whatever afflict you.

verses.book: Register of the Epistles of St. Gregory the Great.
verses.text: For, with the assistance of Divine Grace, we trust that it will not be long before the presumption of the aforesaid excommunicated prevaricator will be more strictly repressed, and your quiet, as you desire, arrive.

verses.book: Register of the Epistles of St. Gregory the Great.
verses.text: We have also by no means omitted to write about his perverseness to our most excellent son the Exarch, who is anxious to commend him to us.

verses.book: Register of the Epistles of St. Gregory the Great.
verses.text: As to the presbyter about whom thy Fraternity has consulted us through the representation of the bearer of these presents, know that after his lapse he cannot by any means remain in, or be restored to, his sacred order.

verses.book: Register of the Epistles of St. Gregory the Great.
verses.text: Still be ought to be somewhat mildly dealt with, inasmuch as he is said to have readily confessed his fault.

verses.book: Register of the Epistles of St. Gregory the Great.
verses.text: Furthermore, this same bearer spoke at the same time of certain privileges of your Church granted by our predecessors.

verses.book: Register of the Epistles of St. Gregory the Great.
verses.text: About the writings thus referred to by your Charity we wish to be more accurately informed.

verses.book: Register of the Epistles of St. Gregory the Great.
verses.text: Or, if any of them are lying in the registry of your Church, it is necessary that copies of them be transmitted hither; that we may be able with willing mind to renew whatever concerns reverence for your dignity or the genius of the aforesaid Church.

verses.book: Register of the Epistles of St. Gregory the Great.
verses.text: If our common son, the glorious lord Marcellus, should be minded to come hither, urgently persuade him to do so; for on all accounts I desire to see him.

verses.book: Register of the Epistles of St. Gregory the Great.
verses.text: But, if he should choose to remain where he is, do you so exhibit yourselves to him in beseeming charity that you may be able to respond, as becomes you, to the affection which he has towards you.

verses.book: Register of the Epistles of St. Gregory the Great.
verses.text: May Almighty God keep and protect you with the gift of His grace, and enflame your heart to do the things that are well pleasing to Him.
